mechanism: Each platform capability has a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow that owns its complete deployment — Azure resources via a deployment stack and downstream config via Bicep outputs. No capability's resources are provisioned outside its own workflow.
why: Capabilities without deployment autonomy accumulate hidden circular dependencies — changes to one require coordinating across the dependency graph, blocking independent development.

related.note: Access control deploys independently — roles update without coordinating with policy or management groups.
BigBang sequences autonomous capabilities into a full bootstrap — independence allows composition without coupling.
Region injected per-capability from the same GitHub variable — no shared deployment coordinating placement.
Each capability deploys independently to oases-test before oases-prod — validation is per-capability.
Each capability's stack uses deleteAll — code is the complete source of truth per capability.
Each capability has its own trigger+template workflow pair.
Shared config consumed without cross-capability dependencies.

violations: Bicep module shared between two capability deployment stacks — couples their redeployment.
